steps.title: Find what the narrator was supposed to do
steps.content: The passage states: “I was supposed to memorize the whole thing.” That expectation — performing from memory without looking at the sheet music — is the standard being violated when the narrator glances up to see the next notes. “Cheating” refers specifically to breaking that rule.

steps.title: Eliminate each wrong choice, then confirm the answer
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘She was violating an expectation about how to perform the piece.’: The expectation (memorize and play without looking) is stated directly in the text; glancing at the notes violates it. ✓
  • Choice B — ‘She was gaining an unfair advantage over other contestants in the talent show.’: Nothing in the passage links looking at the music to gaining an advantage over other performers; the contest is never the focus. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘She was deceiving her piano teacher.’: No teacher is present in the scene, and the narrator makes no mention of hiding her behavior from anyone. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘She was lying to herself about her musical ability.’: The narrator daydreams about being elsewhere but never claims or denies having a certain level of ability. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
